title: UNICEF Gender Equity Officer Volunteer Opportunity in India 2026: Join UNICEF and Advance Gender Equality Across India
summary: The United Nations Children s Fund (UNICEF), through the United Nations Volunteers (UNV) programme, is inviting qualified professionals in India to apply for the position of Gender Equity Officer . This prestigious full-time national volunteer assignment offers an opportunity to contribute directly to advancing gender equality, social inclusion, and equitable development outcomes for children, adolescents, and communities across India. Based in New Delhi, this 12-month assignment places successful candidates at the heart of UNICEF India s efforts to mainstream gender equality across programmes, policies, and institutional operations. The selected volunteer will work closely with UNICEF s Gender and Development Specialist and support the implementation of gender-focused initiatives across multiple programme sections and field offices nationwide. For professionals passionate about gender equity, social justice, child rights, women s empowerment, and sustainable development, this opportunity provides a unique platform to gain hands-on experience within one of the world s leading international development organizations. About UNICEF United Nations Children s Fund (UNICEF) is the world s leading organization dedicated to protecting and promoting the rights of every child. UNICEF works in more than 190 countries and territories to ensure that children have access to: Quality education Healthcare services Protection from violence and exploitation Nutrition and food security Safe water and sanitation Equal opportunities for growth and development At the core of UNICEF s mission is a commitment to equity, ensuring that the most disadvantaged and marginalized children receive the support they need to reach their full potential. Why Gender Equality Matters at UNICEF Gender equality is a central pillar of UNICEF s programming and institutional strategy. The UNICEF India Country Programme Document (2023–2027) identifies gender equality and equity as cross-cutting priorities that influence all areas of programming and operations. UNICEF s gender work is guided by: UNICEF Gender Policy (2021–2030) UNICEF Strategic Plan (2022–2025) UNICEF Strategic Plan (2026–2029) Gender Equality Action Plan (GEAP) 2022–2025 Gender Equality Action Plan (GEAP) 2026–2029 The Gender Equity Officer will contribute to ensuring that these priorities are effectively integrated across UNICEF India s programmes and state-level interventions. Position Overview Job Title Gender Equity Officer Organization UNICEF Duty Station New Delhi , India Assignment Type National UN Volunteer Assignment Duration 12 Months Expected Start Date July 1, 2026 Work Schedule Full-Time Work Modality Onsite Number of Positions 1 Application Deadline 17 June 2026 Key Responsibilities The selected Gender Equity Officer will support UNICEF India s gender programming efforts across headquarters and state offices. 1. Programme Implementation Support The volunteer will: Support implementation of gender work plans under the 2023–2027 Country Programme Assist in coordinating gender-related activities Provide technical support to gender focal points Facilitate collaboration across programme sections Participate in regular coordination meetings 2. Knowledge Management Responsibilities include: Supporting programme documentation Maintaining knowledge-sharing systems Facilitating communication among gender focal points Contributing to publications and reports Strengthening information management processes 3. Reporting and Monitoring The volunteer will: Contribute to country, regional, and global reporting requirements Monitor gender-related programme results Track gender marker implementation Coordinate data collection from programme sections Support reporting on gender equality outcomes 4. Documentation and Analysis This area involves: Preparing briefing documents Supporting programme reviews Assisting in annual reporting processes Developing analytical reports Documenting lessons learned and best practices 5. Strategic Support The officer will assist with: Drafting presentations Preparing talking points Developing funding proposals Creating briefing notes Supporting strategic communication efforts Qualifications and Educational Requirements Applicants must possess: Minimum Educational Requirement A Bachelor s Degree in: Social Sciences Development Studies Mass Communication Related fields Preferred Qualification Candidates holding a Master s Degree in relevant disciplines will be given preference. Required Professional Experience Applicants must have: At least three years of professional experience at the national level Relevant experience may include: Gender programming Social development Community development Social policy Women s empowerment Development cooperation Human rights programming Youth and adolescent development Experience with: United Nations agencies International organizations Development partners is considered an added advantage. Language Requirements Applicants must demonstrate: Required Fluent English Required Working knowledge of Hindi Strong written and verbal communication skills in both languages are highly desirable. Essential Skills and Competencies UNICEF is seeking professionals who possess strong technical, interpersonal, and organizational skills. Professional Competencies Applicants should demonstrate: Professionalism Integrity Accountability Initiative Sound judgment Confidentiality Resourcefulness Communication Skills Candidates should have: Excellent report writing abilities Strong presentation skills Advanced drafting skills Effective stakeholder engagement capabilities Technical Skills The ideal candidate should possess: Knowledge of gender equality programming Understanding of social development issues Experience in programme coordination Monitoring and reporting skills Documentation and research abilities Digital Skills Applicants should be proficient in: Microsoft Word Microsoft Excel Microsoft PowerPoint Email communication Internet research Database management Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for professionals working in: Gender Equality Women s Rights Social Work Community Development Public Policy International Development Child Protection Human Rights Education Youth Development Communication for Development It is particularly suitable for individuals seeking hands-on experience within the United Nations system while contributing to meaningful social impact. Volunteer Benefits and Financial Support Although categorized as a volunteer assignment, the position comes with substantial financial and professional benefits. Monthly Volunteer Living Allowance Successful candidates will receive: INR 70,158.41 per month This allowance is intended to cover: Housing Utilities Daily living expenses Local transportation Entry Lump Sum Volunteers will receive: USD 350 (equivalent in INR) upon commencement of the assignment. Relocation Support Eligible volunteers may receive: USD 175 at the start and end of the assignment if relocating from outside commuting distance. Exit Allowance Upon successful completion: USD 120 per month served will be paid as an exit allowance. Insurance Coverage Comprehensive coverage includes: Health insurance Life insurance Permanent disability insurance Malicious acts insurance Health insurance extends to up to three dependents. Leave Entitlement Volunteers receive: 2.5 days of annual leave per month Learning and Career Development Participants gain access to: UNV learning resources Professional development opportunities Training programmes Career advancement support Living and Working in New Delhi The assignment is based in New Delhi, India s capital city and one of the country s most important political, economic, and cultural hubs. Living in New Delhi offers: Exposure to diverse cultures and communities Access to international organizations Professional networking opportunities Rich historical and cultural experiences Modern infrastructure and services The city provides a dynamic environment for professionals interested in development, public policy, and international cooperation. Inclusivity and Equal Opportunity UNICEF and UNV strongly encourage applications from diverse candidates. The organizations are committed to: Gender equality Inclusion Diversity Equal opportunity employment Disability inclusion Human rights protection Reasonable accommodation is available for qualified candidates with disabilities throughout the recruitment and assignment process. Why You Should Consider This Opportunity Working with UNICEF provides an opportunity to: Contribute to national gender equality goals Support child rights and social justice initiatives Gain UN system experience Build expertise in development programming Work with leading development professionals Expand your professional network Enhance your career prospects in international development The Gender Equity Officer position offers both meaningful impact and valuable professional growth for individuals committed to advancing gender equality and social inclusion. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their applications through the United Nations Volunteers (UNV) platform before the closing date. Apply Here Application Deadline 17 June 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible to avoid last-minute technical challenges and to ensure all supporting information is submitted successfully. For more information on this job opportunity visit here Discover more volunteering opportunities on OFY here The post UNICEF Gender Equity Officer Volunteer Opportunity in India 2026: Join UNICEF and Advance Gender Equality Across India appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Letters to Strangers Mental Health Scholarships 2026: Win Up to $8,000 for Mental Health Advocacy and Educational Support
summary: Mental health remains one of the most pressing global challenges facing young people today. Yet, despite growing awareness, many students and advocates continue to face barriers in accessing support, funding, and opportunities to further their education and impact. To help address these challenges, the global youth-led nonprofit organization offers its annual Mental Health Scholarships, designed to recognize and support students who are creating positive change in mental health advocacy and those who have personally faced obstacles in obtaining mental health care. The Letters to Strangers Mental Health Scholarships have become a recognized opportunity for students who are passionate about mental wellness, advocacy, community impact, and educational advancement. Through two distinct scholarship tracks—the Changemaker Scholarship and the Warrior Scholarship—the organization provides financial assistance, recognition, and resources to support students on their academic and personal journeys. About Letters to Strangers Letters to Strangers is a global youth-for-youth nonprofit organization focused on mental health awareness, mental illness destigmatization, and increasing access to quality mental health care. The organization engages young people through advocacy, education, storytelling, community-building, and peer support initiatives. Its scholarship program is one of its flagship efforts to empower students making meaningful contributions to mental health awareness worldwide. Scholarship Opportunities Available The Letters to Strangers Scholarship Program currently features two major awards. 1. Mental Health Changemaker Scholarship The Changemaker Scholarship is designed for students who have demonstrated a strong commitment to mental health advocacy and community impact. Scholarship Benefits Recipients may receive: One Grand Prize of $3,000 USD Nine additional finalist awards Funding support for: Tuition expenses Housing and board Educational materials Project implementation costs Advocacy initiatives Academic development activities In total, the Changemaker Scholarship distributes up to $8,000 USD in scholarship funding and awards. 2. Mental Health Warrior Scholarship The Warrior Scholarship recognizes individuals who have personally faced barriers to accessing mental health support and services. Scholarship Benefit One Grand Prize of $3,000 USD Unlike traditional scholarship competitions, the Warrior Scholarship winner is selected through a process intended to reduce pressure on applicants and encourage authentic participation. Why This Scholarship Matters Mental health challenges affect millions of students worldwide. Unfortunately, access to treatment, support services, and educational opportunities related to mental health remains unequal. The Letters to Strangers Scholarships aim to: Support mental health advocates Reduce barriers to education Empower youth leaders Promote mental health awareness Encourage community engagement Amplify underrepresented voices Create positive social impact Advance mental health equity By investing in young leaders and students, the program helps build a stronger and more inclusive future for mental health advocacy globally. Who Should Apply? The scholarship is particularly suitable for: High school graduates entering higher education Undergraduate students Graduate students Vocational and technical students Mental health advocates Community organizers Youth leaders Social impact innovators Individuals with lived mental health experiences Students pursuing careers in psychology, counseling, public health, social work, medicine, education, or related fields Eligibility Requirements Changemaker Scholarship Eligibility Applicants generally must: Be incoming or currently enrolled students at an accredited post-secondary institution Demonstrate involvement in mental health advocacy, awareness, education, or related initiatives Show commitment to creating positive change within their communities Additional consideration may be given to: Applicants under 26 years old Individuals from underrepresented groups in mental health spaces Students affiliated with Letters to Strangers chapters or partner organizations. Warrior Scholarship Eligibility Applicants generally must: Be at least 18 years old Have experienced barriers to accessing mental health services Demonstrate how those experiences have influenced their educational and personal journey Additional consideration may be given to younger applicants and individuals affiliated with Letters to Strangers programs. Scholarship Selection Criteria Successful applicants are typically evaluated based on: For Changemaker Applicants Leadership in mental health advocacy Community involvement Impact of projects and initiatives Personal commitment to mental wellness Vision for future contributions Educational aspirations For Warrior Applicants Personal resilience Experiences navigating mental health barriers Commitment to growth and wellbeing Educational goals Authenticity and reflection The program values genuine experiences and meaningful impact over formal titles or extensive achievements. Scholarship Application Materials Applicants may be required to submit: Completed online application form Personal statement or essay Evidence of mental health advocacy involvement (for Changemaker applicants) Proof of enrollment or admission Supporting documentation where applicable Specific requirements may vary during each scholarship cycle. Applicants should review official application guidelines when applications reopen. Additional Benefits Beyond Funding One unique aspect of the Letters to Strangers Scholarship Program is that applicants may receive access to mental wellbeing resources regardless of whether they win a scholarship. Benefits may include: Mental wellness materials Educational resources Community engagement opportunities Advocacy support Access to a global network of changemakers This approach reflects the organization s mission to support mental wellbeing beyond financial assistance alone. Scholarship Judges and Mentors The scholarship program has featured judges and leaders from diverse sectors, including: Social entrepreneurship Public health Education Mental health advocacy Policy and governance Media and storytelling These professionals help identify applicants who demonstrate leadership, compassion, and a commitment to improving mental health outcomes. Application Deadline Applications for the 2026 scholarship cycle are currently closed. Applicants should monitor the official Letters to Strangers website for announcements regarding future scholarship rounds and reopening dates. How to Stay Updated Interested applicants should; Apply Here You may also subscribe to the organization s newsletter to receive updates on scholarship openings, mental health resources, and advocacy opportunities. Final Thoughts The Letters to Strangers Mental Health Scholarships provide more than financial support—they recognize the courage, advocacy, and resilience of students working to improve mental health outcomes in their communities. Whether you are leading awareness campaigns, supporting peers, conducting research, or overcoming personal challenges related to mental health access, these scholarships offer a meaningful opportunity to receive recognition and educational support. Students passionate about mental wellness, social impact, and community leadership should keep an eye on future application cycles and prepare early to maximize their chances of success. For more information on this scholarship visit here Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY here The post Letters to Strangers Mental Health Scholarships 2026: Win Up to $8,000 for Mental Health Advocacy and Educational Support appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Nkabom Internship Programme 2026: Apply for a Fully Structured 6-Month Agribusiness Internship Across All 16 Regions of Ghana
summary: The Nkabom Internship Programme is offering a unique opportunity for young Ghanaians to gain hands-on experience in the country s rapidly growing agri-food sector through a structured six-month internship programme. Implemented by the Nkabom Collaborative at the University of Ghana in partnership with the Chamber of Agribusiness Ghana (CAG), the initiative seeks to connect talented youth with verified host enterprises across all 16 regions of Ghana. The programme is designed to bridge the gap between education and employment while creating pathways for entrepreneurship within Ghana s agriculture and agribusiness ecosystem. Through practical placements, mentorship, business exposure, and career development opportunities, participants will gain the skills and networks needed to thrive in the agri-food industry. Whether you are a recent graduate seeking employment opportunities or an aspiring entrepreneur looking to launch an agribusiness venture, the Nkabom Internship Programme provides a platform to develop professionally while contributing to Ghana s agricultural transformation agenda. About the Nkabom Internship Programme The Nkabom Internship Programme is a youth-focused initiative aimed at creating meaningful opportunities in Ghana s agriculture and food systems. Participants will be placed in verified host organizations where they will receive structured workplace experience and practical training. The programme is implemented through a strategic collaboration involving: Nkabom Collaborative at the University of Ghana Chamber of Agribusiness Ghana (CAG) Mastercard Foundation By linking young professionals with agribusiness enterprises, the programme seeks to improve employability, foster entrepreneurship, and strengthen the future workforce of Ghana s agricultural sector. Why the Nkabom Internship Programme Matters Agriculture remains one of the most important sectors of Ghana s economy, yet many young graduates struggle to gain practical experience needed by employers. The Nkabom Internship Programme addresses this challenge by: Providing real-world work experience Enhancing employability skills Connecting youth with industry professionals Supporting entrepreneurship development Promoting innovation in agriculture Creating pathways to long-term employment Encouraging youth participation in agribusiness The programme recognizes agriculture as more than farming. It encompasses technology, finance, logistics, processing, sustainability, innovation, and entrepreneurship. Internship Tracks Available Applicants can choose between two distinct pathways. Track A: Employment Pathway This track focuses on preparing participants for employment within agribusiness enterprises. Key features include: Six-month internship placement Exposure to industry operations Professional development opportunities Workplace mentoring Employment-focused training Potential transition into formal employment The ultimate objective of Track A is to help participants secure long-term employment opportunities within the agribusiness sector. Track B: Entrepreneurship Pathway Track B is designed for participants interested in starting their own agribusiness ventures. The pathway includes: Six-month internship placement Structured incubation support Business mentorship Entrepreneurial training Access to business development services Potential access to seed grant opportunities Founder-focused support over a 12-month period This track is ideal for individuals with innovative agribusiness ideas and entrepreneurial ambitions. Who Can Apply? To be eligible for the Nkabom Internship Programme, applicants must meet the following requirements: Citizenship Requirement Applicants must: Be Ghanaian citizens Age Requirement Applicants must: Be between 18 and 35 years old Academic Requirement Applicants must be one of the following: Recent graduates (within the last three years) TVET graduates Postgraduate students Availability Requirement Applicants must: Be available for the entire six-month internship duration Placement Locations One of the most attractive aspects of the programme is its nationwide coverage. Internship opportunities are available across: All 16 regions of Ghana Applicants can indicate: Their current region of residence First preferred internship region Second preferred internship region Third preferred internship region Participants may also indicate whether they: Are willing to relocate anywhere in Ghana Prefer placements within their current region Would relocate only if relocation support is provided The programme also encourages applicants to consider opportunities in rural and peri-urban farming communities. Areas of Internship Placement The Nkabom Internship Programme offers placements across a wide range of agribusiness and agri-food sectors. 1. Primary Production Participants may be placed in areas such as: Crop farming (maize, rice, sorghum, millet) Vegetable production Horticulture Fruit production Tree crops Cocoa production Poultry farming Small ruminant farming Dairy production Piggery Aquaculture Fisheries Beekeeping 2. Processing and Manufacturing Potential opportunities include: Grain processing Rice milling Flour production Cassava processing Yam processing Plantain processing Fruit processing Vegetable processing Cocoa processing Chocolate production Dairy processing Meat processing Beverage production Packaging services Branding services 3. Agribusiness Inputs and Mechanization Participants may gain exposure to: Seed production Seed distribution Fertilizer supply Soil amendment services Agrochemical businesses Mechanization services Tractor operations Irrigation services Veterinary services Animal feed businesses 4. Trade, Logistics and Markets Internship opportunities include: Commodity trading Agricultural aggregation Warehousing Inventory management Export operations AfCFTA trade EU market exports US market exports Cold-chain logistics Food distribution systems 5. Agritech and Innovation Emerging technology-focused opportunities include: Digital agriculture platforms Agricultural mobile applications Agricultural marketplaces Data-driven agriculture Artificial intelligence in agriculture Remote sensing technologies Drone services Precision agriculture Mechanization platforms Agri-payment systems 6. Sustainability and Climate Growing opportunities exist in: Climate-smart agriculture Organic farming Regenerative agriculture Renewable energy solutions Solar-powered irrigation Biogas systems Forestry Agroforestry Land restoration 7. Finance and Business Support Services Participants may gain experience in: Agribusiness finance Credit unions Cooperative development Agricultural insurance Risk management Business advisory services Market research Agribusiness consulting Skills and Experience Considered The programme welcomes applicants with a variety of skills and experiences. Examples include: Communication skills Presentation skills Digital literacy Microsoft Office proficiency Marketing experience Sales experience Business development skills Agricultural technical skills Financial literacy Bookkeeping Data analysis Leadership Teamwork Project management Driving skills Language proficiency Previous work experience is not mandatory, making the programme accessible to many young graduates entering the workforce. Required Application Documents Applicants must submit the following documents as a single PDF file: Mandatory Documents Curriculum Vitae (CV) with a maximum length of two pages Preferred Documents Most recent academic transcript Statement of results Optional Documents Recommendation letter from an academic referee Recommendation letter from a professional referee Additional Documents for Entrepreneurship Track Applicants Track B applicants are encouraged to submit: A 1–2 page business concept document A business pitch document Special Consideration Documents Applicants applying under disability priority status may submit: Proof of disability registration from the Ghana Federation of Disability Organisations or other recognized bodies Inclusion and Diversity The programme promotes inclusivity and welcomes applications from various backgrounds. Priority groups may include: Women Persons with disabilities Migrants Internally displaced persons Survivors of gender-based violence Single parents Sole breadwinners First-generation university students Applicants from historically underserved communities Career Development Benefits Participants can expect to gain: Professional Benefits Practical industry experience Improved employability Professional mentorship Workplace readiness skills Networking opportunities Entrepreneurship Benefits Business incubation support Entrepreneurial mentorship Exposure to investors and industry experts Business development guidance Potential seed grant access Long-Term Benefits Enhanced career prospects Strong professional networks Greater understanding of agribusiness value chains Exposure to emerging technologies Opportunities for employment or business creation Data Protection and Privacy All applicant information is handled confidentially and processed according to Ghana s Data Protection Act, 2012 (Act 843). Application data will be used solely for: Application assessment Programme administration Monitoring and evaluation Reporting requirements How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the official Nkabom Internship Programme application form and upload all required supporting documents as a single PDF file. Applicants are encouraged to prepare all documents before beginning the application process to ensure a smooth submission process. Apply Here Application Deadline No application deadline has been stated in the available information. Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible before the application portal closes. Final Thoughts The Nkabom Internship Programme represents a significant opportunity for young Ghanaians to launch successful careers in agribusiness while contributing to national development. With placements available across all regions of Ghana and opportunities spanning agriculture, technology, sustainability, logistics, finance, and entrepreneurship, the programme provides a comprehensive platform for career growth and innovation. Young graduates, TVET graduates, and postgraduate students who are passionate about agriculture, food systems, entrepreneurship, and sustainable development should strongly consider applying for this transformative opportunity. Discover more internship opportunities on OFY, visit here The post Nkabom Internship Programme 2026: Apply for a Fully Structured 6-Month Agribusiness Internship Across All 16 Regions of Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: YLAI 2026-2027 Outbound Exchange Grant: Funding U.S. Business Leaders to Strengthen Partnerships Across Latin America and the Caribbean
summary: Applications are now officially open for the Young Leaders of the Americas Initiative (YLAI) 2026-2027 Outbound Exchange Program, a unique international exchange and grant opportunity designed to help U.S.-based partners continue and expand collaborations established during the 2026 YLAI Fellowship. Sponsored by the U.S. Department of State and implemented by IREX, the YLAI Outbound Exchange provides selected U.S. participants with the opportunity to travel to Latin America and the Caribbean (LAC) for a two-week collaborative project with a 2026 YLAI Alum. The program is designed to strengthen cross-border business partnerships, promote entrepreneurship, build professional networks, and foster economic growth throughout the Western Hemisphere. For organizations and professionals who previously hosted or collaborated with a 2026 YLAI Fellow, this exchange offers an exciting opportunity to deepen relationships, expand market opportunities, and create lasting impact through international collaboration. What is the YLAI Outbound Exchange Program? The YLAI Outbound Exchange Program is a grant-funded initiative that builds upon the relationships and professional partnerships formed during the Young Leaders of the Americas Initiative Fellowship. The program supports U.S.-based participants as they travel to the home countries of 2026 YLAI Alumni throughout Latin America and the Caribbean. During the exchange, participants work together on substantive two-week projects that advance shared business objectives, entrepreneurial development, professional growth, and community impact. The initiative recognizes that some of the most impactful partnerships emerge after the conclusion of fellowship programs. By supporting continued collaboration, the Outbound Exchange strengthens international business relationships while creating opportunities for innovation, learning, and sustainable economic development. About the Young Leaders of the Americas Initiative (YLAI) The Young Leaders of the Americas Initiative is a flagship leadership and entrepreneurship program that empowers emerging entrepreneurs from Latin America and the Caribbean. Through professional placements, leadership training, networking opportunities, and mentorship, YLAI helps entrepreneurs strengthen their ventures while building meaningful partnerships with organizations and businesses in the United States. The Outbound Exchange extends these relationships beyond the fellowship period, enabling both U.S. participants and YLAI Alumni to continue working together on initiatives that create measurable impact. Purpose of the Outbound Exchange The YLAI Outbound Exchange seeks to strengthen entrepreneurial ecosystems and international partnerships across the Western Hemisphere. Key objectives include: Building upon successful partnerships formed during the YLAI Fellowship. Supporting long-term collaboration between U.S. organizations and YLAI Alumni. Expanding business and professional networks. Increasing market access and opportunities. Encouraging knowledge sharing and capacity building. Supporting entrepreneurship and innovation. Promoting economic prosperity in local communities. Strengthening mutual understanding between the United States and Latin America and the Caribbean. The program is designed to create benefits for both participants while generating positive outcomes for broader entrepreneurial communities. Benefits of the YLAI Outbound Exchange Grant Selected participants will gain access to numerous professional and organizational benefits, including: International Collaboration Opportunities Participants can continue developing relationships established during the fellowship and transform initial collaborations into long-term partnerships. Expanded Business Networks The exchange enables participants to connect with entrepreneurs, business leaders, community organizations, and stakeholders throughout Latin America and the Caribbean. Professional Development The two-week project experience provides opportunities to learn from different business environments, cultural contexts, and entrepreneurial ecosystems. Market Expansion Opportunities Organizations may identify new partnerships, customers, suppliers, investment opportunities, or business collaborations through expanded regional engagement. Community Impact Projects are designed to support local entrepreneurial communities while contributing to economic development and innovation. Cross-Cultural Learning Participants gain firsthand exposure to diverse perspectives, business practices, and approaches to entrepreneurship. Who Should Apply? The program is intended for U.S.-based organizations and professionals that collaborated with a 2026 YLAI Fellow during the fellowship program. Ideal applicants may include: Small business owners. Startup founders. Business executives. Nonprofit leaders. Social entrepreneurs. Community organizations. Economic development professionals. Innovation and entrepreneurship support organizations. Fellowship host organizations. Business mentors and advisors. Applicants should have an existing relationship with a 2026 YLAI Alum and a clear vision for how the partnership can be strengthened through the exchange. Eligibility Requirements Successful applicants must demonstrate: Existing Collaboration Applicants should provide evidence of productive collaboration with a 2026 YLAI Alum during the fellowship period. Partnership Development The proposed exchange project should clearly explain how the grant will strengthen and expand the existing partnership. Community Engagement Participants must travel to the YLAI Alum s country and engage directly with local entrepreneurial communities. Economic Impact Projects should contribute to economic prosperity, entrepreneurship development, or business growth within the region. Professional Advancement The proposed activities should support the professional goals of both the U.S. participant and the YLAI Alum. Applicants are strongly encouraged to carefully review all official eligibility requirements before submitting their applications. What Makes a Competitive Application? Strong applications will typically demonstrate: A proven history of collaboration. Clearly defined project goals. Measurable outcomes and impact. Long-term sustainability. Benefits for both partners. Community engagement strategies. Entrepreneurship-focused activities. Regional economic impact. Capacity-building opportunities. Strong implementation plans. Applicants should clearly communicate how their project will create meaningful outcomes that extend beyond the two-week exchange period. Required Application Materials Applicants should download and review the following documents before applying: Application Checklist Application Instructions Draft Budget Template Draft Work Plan Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) Document Terms and Conditions Agreement Carefully preparing these materials in advance can help applicants submit stronger and more competitive proposals. Important Dates Application Opening Date June 8, 2026 Optional Information Session June 16, 2026 3:30 PM – 4:30 PM EDT Applicants can join an optional Zoom Q A session to learn more about the grant opportunity, application process, and eligibility requirements. Application Deadline Monday, July 6, 2026 11:59 PM EDT Late applications may not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to submit their materials well before the deadline. How to Apply Interested applicants should: Step 1 Review all application guidance documents and eligibility requirements. Step 2 Discuss the opportunity with their 2026 YLAI Alum partner and develop a collaborative project concept. Step 3 Prepare all required application materials, including work plans and budget documents. Step 4 Submit the completed application through the official application portal before the deadline. Click here for more and to apply Why This Opportunity Matters The YLAI Outbound Exchange represents more than a travel grant. It is an investment in long-term international collaboration, entrepreneurship, innovation, and economic development throughout the Americas. By supporting meaningful partnerships between U.S. organizations and YLAI Alumni, the program helps create opportunities that extend beyond borders, strengthen entrepreneurial ecosystems, and foster sustainable growth across the Western Hemisphere. Organizations that have already built successful relationships through the YLAI Fellowship should strongly consider applying to continue their collaboration and maximize the impact of their partnership. Click here for more global grant opportunities The post YLAI 2026-2027 Outbound Exchange Grant: Funding U.S. Business Leaders to Strengthen Partnerships Across Latin America and the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: MMEG Scholarships for Latin America 2026: Fully Funded Support for Women Advancing Education and Social Change
summary: Applications are now officially open for the MMEG Scholarships for Latin America 2026, a prestigious funding opportunity designed to support women committed to improving the lives of women and children through education, leadership, and community impact. The Margaret McNamara Education Grants (MMEG) program provides financial assistance to women from developing and middle-income countries who are pursuing higher education and who demonstrate a strong commitment to social development and gender equality. Women across Latin America who are currently enrolled in eligible universities can now apply for scholarship support for undergraduate, master’s, doctoral, and postdoctoral studies. The application period runs from June 1, 2026, to September 30, 2026 (11:59 PM Eastern Standard Time). About the MMEG Scholarship Program The Margaret McNamara Education Grants (MMEG) is a non-profit organization established to support women pursuing higher education in order to improve the quality of life in their communities. The program specifically invests in women who are passionate about creating long-term social impact for women and children. The scholarship aims to empower women leaders, researchers, educators, policymakers, and professionals who intend to contribute to sustainable development and community transformation after completing their studies. Applicants selected for the scholarship become part of a global network of women leaders and changemakers working across multiple sectors and disciplines. What the MMEG Scholarship Supports The MMEG Scholarship supports women enrolled in: Undergraduate/Pregraduate programs Master’s degree programs Doctoral programs Postdoctoral studies The scholarship is intended to help cover educational and academic-related expenses while students continue their studies at participating universities in Latin America. Applicants are required to submit their estimated expenses in U.S. Dollars during the application process. Application Deadline Opening Date: June 1, 2026 Closing Date: September 30, 2026 (11:59 PM EST) Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early and prepare all supporting documents before the deadline. Successful applicants will be notified individually in January 2027, while results will also be published on the official MMEG website in mid-January. Eligible Universities in Latin America Applicants must already be enrolled and registered — not merely accepted — at one of the following participating institutions: Participating Universities Centro Agronómico Tropical de Investigación y Enseñanza (CATIE), Costa Rica FLACSO Argentina Costa Rica Ecuador El Salvador Guatemala Honduras Mexico Panama Paraguay Dominican Republic INCAE Costa Rica Nicaragua Universidad Austral, Argentina Pontifical Catholic University of Peru (Universidad Católica del Perú), Peru Universidad de Buenos Aires (UBA), Argentina Universidad de los Andes, Colombia Universidad Iberoamericana, Mexico Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México (UNAM), Mexico Universidad Nacional de Colombia, Colombia Universidad Rafael Landívar, Guatemala Eligibility Criteria for the MMEG Scholarship 2026 To qualify for the MMEG Latin America Scholarship, applicants must meet all the following requirements: Age Requirement Applicants must: Identify as women Be at least 25 years old by the application deadline Citizenship Requirement Applicants must: Be citizens of a country eligible under the MMEG program Enrollment Requirement Applicants must: Be currently enrolled and registered in an eligible academic program Study either full-time or part-time Take at least two courses per academic period Applicants must already be actively studying at one of the participating universities listed above. Degree Levels Accepted Applicants can be enrolled in: Undergraduate programs Master’s programs PhD/Doctoral programs Postdoctoral programs Academic Continuation Requirement After receiving the scholarship, recipients must continue their enrollment during the January–July academic period of the following year. Graduation Timeline Applicants must: Have an expected graduation date no more than three years after receiving the scholarship Institutional Restrictions Applicants must not have family ties to: Employees Spouses Retirees of the following organizations: The World Bank International Monetary Fund (IMF) Inter-American Development Bank (IDB) Members of the MMEG Board Commitment to Social Impact One of the strongest components of the MMEG Scholarship is its emphasis on community impact. The scholarship specifically supports women who intend to: Return to developing countries after graduation, or Continue living in developing countries to work toward improving the lives of: Women Girls Children Vulnerable communities Applicants are expected to demonstrate leadership potential, social responsibility, and a long-term commitment to development and equality. Important Application Requirement: AI-Generated Essays Not Allowed MMEG has clearly stated that all application essays must be written independently by applicants. The organization emphasizes authenticity and personal storytelling. According to the scholarship guidelines: Essays generated using Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools will lead to disqualification. Applicants are encouraged to share genuine personal experiences and use their authentic voice throughout the application. This requirement makes it extremely important for applicants to prepare original essays and personal statements. Application Checklist and Preparation Tips Before starting the online application, applicants are advised to review: Eligibility Criteria Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) Application Checklist Preparing these documents early can significantly improve the quality and completeness of the application. Why the MMEG Scholarship Matters The MMEG Scholarship is more than just financial support. It is an investment in women who are working toward educational advancement and societal transformation. Recipients become part of an international network of women leaders dedicated to: Education Social justice Gender equality Economic development Community empowerment The scholarship also provides visibility and recognition for women pursuing impactful careers in academia, policy, development, healthcare, education, and social innovation. Final Thoughts The MMEG Scholarships for Latin America 2026 represent a major opportunity for women pursuing higher education while working toward meaningful social impact in their communities. Women leaders, researchers, educators, and changemakers across Latin America are encouraged to apply and take advantage of this opportunity to advance their education and strengthen their contribution to society. With a strong focus on women’s empowerment, community leadership, and educational advancement, the MMEG Scholarship continues to support the next generation of impactful women leaders across the region. Learn more and apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the MMEG Scholarships for Latin America 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post MMEG Scholarships for Latin America 2026: Fully Funded Support for Women Advancing Education and Social Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: United Nations University Science-Policy Visiting Fellow – Data Governance Fellowship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland (CHF 5,500 Monthly Stipend)
summary: Researchers and policy professionals from the Global South now have an exceptional opportunity to contribute to global discussions on digital governance, development, and emerging technologies through the United Nations University (UNU) Science-Policy Visiting Fellowship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland. Hosted by the United Nations University Centre for Policy Research (UNU-CPR) in collaboration with the Geneva Science-Policy Interface (GSPI), this prestigious six-month fellowship offers selected fellows the chance to conduct policy-relevant research, engage directly with international organizations, and influence global conversations on equitable data governance. The fellowship is specifically designed to strengthen Global South participation in international science-policy processes while supporting research that contributes to sustainable development, digital inclusion, and equitable governance frameworks. About the United Nations University (UNU) The United Nations University serves as the academic and research arm of the United Nations system. For more than five decades, UNU has generated policy-relevant research and practical solutions to some of the world s most pressing challenges. With researchers and institutes operating across multiple countries, UNU supports evidence-based policymaking related to: Sustainable development Human security Economic development Science and technology Peace and governance Climate and environmental challenges Through its global network, UNU collaborates with governments, UN agencies, research institutions, and policymakers to advance the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). About UNU Centre for Policy Research (UNU-CPR) The United Nations University Centre for Policy Research is one of the leading policy research institutes within the UNU system. Headquartered in New York with an additional office in Geneva, UNU-CPR focuses on: Innovative policy solutions Evidence-based research International cooperation Multilateral governance Sustainable development The Geneva office was established to strengthen engagement with the international policy community and support collaboration between scientific experts and decision-makers. About the Geneva Science-Policy Interface (GSPI) The Geneva Science-Policy Interface is an independent platform dedicated to promoting science-informed policymaking. Established by the University of Geneva with support from the Swiss Federal Department of Foreign Affairs, GSPI helps bridge the gap between: Scientific institutions Researchers International organizations Diplomats Policymakers The organization facilitates meaningful collaboration that enables scientific evidence to inform international policy decisions. Fellowship Focus: Data Governance for Development The selected fellow will work on a major international research initiative titled: Data Governance for Development: From Asymmetry to Equitable Stewardship. This project is being undertaken in collaboration with the Secretariat of the Commission on Science and Technology for Development (CSTD). The fellowship will explore how inequalities in data ownership, access, infrastructure, and governance contribute to development challenges worldwide. Particular attention will be given to: Data governance frameworks Digital cooperation Inclusive technology policies Development-focused digital transformation Equitable access to digital resources Protection of vulnerable and marginalized populations The research will contribute directly to discussions taking place within the United Nations system regarding the future of data governance. Why Data Governance Matters As data becomes one of the world s most valuable strategic resources, many developing nations face challenges related to: Limited data infrastructure Unequal access to digital technologies Insufficient governance capacity Dependence on external digital platforms Underrepresentation in global digital policymaking The fellowship seeks to address these challenges by developing policy recommendations that promote: Inclusive digital cooperation Fair data governance Sustainable development Digital equity Shared prosperity The research will also examine how data collection practices may unintentionally reinforce inequalities affecting: Women Rural communities Marginalized populations Developing countries Vulnerable social groups Responsibilities of the Visiting Fellow The selected fellow will undertake a broad range of research and policy activities. 1. Research and Analysis The fellow will: Develop an analytical policy report on equitable data governance. Conduct research on global digital governance frameworks. Examine data-related inequalities affecting developing nations. Contribute to the CSTD issues note. Develop forward-looking policy recommendations. Analyze implementation efforts related to the Global Digital Compact. 2. Science-Policy Engagement The fellow will: Engage with Geneva-based science and technology stakeholders. Collaborate with organizations such as: UNCTAD CSTD UN entities Research institutions Deliver presentations and policy briefings. Facilitate stakeholder engagement activities. Organize one to two workshops involving diplomats, scientists, and civil society representatives. 3. Knowledge Development The fellow will: Produce an academic article based on fellowship research. Contribute to internal knowledge-sharing activities. Support the development of future fellowship initiatives. Strengthen international science-policy collaboration. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will receive substantial support, including: Financial Benefits CHF 5,500 monthly remuneration CHF 3,000 lump-sum allowance Travel costs to Geneva covered Return travel home covered Visa support provided Professional Benefits Opportunity to work directly with United Nations entities Access to international policy discussions Exposure to global science-policy networks Collaboration with leading researchers and policymakers Experience influencing international development agendas Opportunity to publish policy and academic research Networking Benefits Direct engagement with diplomats Collaboration with UN experts Access to Geneva s international policy ecosystem Participation in workshops and global discussions Connections with leading development and technology professionals Required Qualifications Applicants should possess strong academic and professional backgrounds relevant to the fellowship. Educational Background A PhD is strongly preferred in areas such as: Economics Development Economics Business Administration Public Policy Governance Political Economy Data Science Artificial Intelligence Quantitative Modeling Candidates with a Master s degree and significant relevant professional experience may also be considered. Experience Requirements Applicants should demonstrate: At least three years of policy-relevant research experience. Experience working on science and technology policy issues. Strong analytical and writing skills. Ability to communicate complex topics to policy audiences. Experience working with diverse stakeholders. Additional advantages include: Familiarity with data governance frameworks. Knowledge of digital development policies. Understanding of internet governance processes. Experience organizing workshops and stakeholder consultations. Knowledge of challenges facing developing countries. Language Requirements Applicants must possess: Professional-level fluency in English. Additional UN languages are considered an asset. Duration and Location Fellowship Duration Six months Non-renewable Location Geneva, Switzerland This is a fully in-person position requiring regular attendance in the office and participation in meetings and events. Remote work is not permitted. Application Process Interested candidates must submit: A current CV in English (maximum two pages). A cover letter in English (maximum one page). Responses to position-specific application questions. The cover letter should clearly explain: Motivation for applying. Relevant qualifications and experience. Potential contribution to the fellowship. Long-term career goals. Important Application Deadline Applications must be submitted no later than: 17 June 2026 at 6:00 PM Geneva Time Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, making early submission highly advisable. Late applications will not be accepted. Apply Here Why You Should Apply This fellowship provides a rare opportunity for Global South researchers and policy professionals to influence international discussions on one of the most important development challenges of the digital era. By joining UNU-CPR and GSPI, fellows will gain access to world-class research networks, engage directly with international policymakers, and contribute to shaping global approaches to equitable data governance. For professionals passionate about technology, development, governance, and international cooperation, the UNU Science-Policy Visiting Fellowship represents a unique pathway to advance both career growth and global impact. For more information, visit the official website. title: LEGO Student Worker, Mould Supply Planning 2026 in Denmark: Gain Hands-On Supply Chain Experience with a Global Brand
summary: Are you a university student looking to gain valuable real-world experience in supply chain management, planning, operations, and data management while working for one of the world s most recognized and innovative companies? The LEGO Group is inviting applications for its Student Worker, Mould Supply Planning position based in Billund, Denmark. This exciting opportunity allows students to contribute directly to operational planning processes while gaining practical experience in supply chain functions that support the production and delivery of LEGO products worldwide. As part of the Mould Supply Planning team, successful candidates will work alongside experienced professionals, learn industry best practices, and develop skills that can accelerate their future careers. If you are passionate about problem-solving, data analysis, planning, and collaboration, this position offers an exceptional opportunity to build your professional experience with a globally respected employer. About The LEGO Group The LEGO Group is one of the world s leading toy manufacturers and a globally recognized brand known for creativity, innovation, learning, and play. Headquartered in Billund, Denmark, LEGO has inspired generations through products that encourage imagination, problem-solving, and creativity. The company s culture is deeply rooted in collaboration, diversity, innovation, and continuous learning. LEGO emphasizes employee wellbeing, professional development, flexibility, and creating an environment where individuals can thrive both personally and professionally. Working at LEGO provides exposure to international teams, world-class processes, and a workplace culture that values curiosity, creativity, and teamwork. About the Student Worker, Mould Supply Planning Role The Student Worker position is part of LEGO s Mould Supply Planning team, a dedicated group responsible for managing critical planning processes that ensure moulds, master data, and parts move efficiently throughout the company s value chain. The team plays an essential role in supporting production operations by ensuring that planning information remains accurate, updated, and aligned with organizational needs. This role offers students practical exposure to: Supply chain operations Production planning Data management Cost tracking Process optimization Business operations Cross-functional collaboration The position is ideal for students seeking hands-on experience that complements their academic studies. Key Responsibilities As a Student Worker in Mould Supply Planning, you will contribute to several important operational activities. 1. Support Weekly Operational Planning Tasks You will help ensure the accuracy of operational data used in planning and cost management processes. This includes supporting workflows related to mould economics and resource tracking. Responsibilities include: Monitoring operational planning data Supporting cost-related reporting activities Ensuring data consistency and accuracy Assisting with information flow across systems 2. Update Master Data and BOM Changes The role involves supporting updates to master data records and Bills of Materials (BOMs), helping maintain accurate planning information throughout the organization. Tasks include: Updating planning records Supporting system data maintenance Assisting with BOM modifications Ensuring information accuracy 3. Assist with Delay Reports and Planning Updates You will support planning teams by helping maintain and update reports that track project timelines and operational performance. Key activities include: Updating planning reports Monitoring operational delays Supporting planning documentation Assisting with performance tracking 4. Provide Administrative and Meeting Support The student worker will assist with departmental coordination activities. This may include: Preparing meeting materials Supporting team events Organizing planning documentation Assisting with internal communication activities 5. Collaborate Across Departments The role requires working with multiple stakeholders across the LEGO organization. You will: Communicate with colleagues from various departments Support collaborative planning activities Participate in team discussions Contribute to process improvements About the Mould Supply Planning Team The Mould Supply Planning team consists of approximately ten dedicated professionals responsible for ensuring smooth planning processes across LEGO s production ecosystem. The team focuses on: Mould cost management Master data maintenance Part planning Process efficiency Value chain optimization Team members work together to ensure that moulds and production components are available when needed, supporting the timely delivery of LEGO products around the world. The environment is collaborative, supportive, and focused on continuous improvement. Who Should Apply? LEGO is looking for motivated and analytical students who are eager to gain professional experience while continuing their studies. Ideal candidates are individuals who: Enjoy solving practical business challenges Have strong attention to detail Thrive in collaborative environments Are comfortable working with data Have an interest in operations and supply chain management Can balance academic and professional responsibilities Required Qualifications Applicants should currently be pursuing a university degree in a relevant field. Relevant disciplines include: Supply Chain Management Economics Data Management Business Administration Operations Management Industrial Engineering Logistics Related fields Essential Skills Successful candidates should demonstrate: Analytical Skills Ability to analyze information effectively Strong problem-solving capabilities Data interpretation skills Logical thinking Technical Skills Proficiency in Microsoft Excel Proficiency in Microsoft PowerPoint General Microsoft Office knowledge Ability to work with business systems and databases Communication Skills Strong written communication Strong verbal communication Ability to collaborate with diverse stakeholders Professional interpersonal skills Organizational Skills Ability to manage multiple priorities Structured working style Attention to detail Time management capabilities Language Skills Applicants must be fluent in English, both written and spoken. Benefits of Working at LEGO LEGO offers a wide range of benefits that support employee wellbeing, professional growth, and work-life balance. Family Care Leave Employees have access to enhanced leave options that support important family and life events. Insurance Coverage LEGO provides: Life insurance Disability insurance Additional protection benefits These programs help provide peace of mind and financial security. Wellbeing Support LEGO prioritizes employee wellbeing through: Physical wellness resources Mental health support Community-building activities Creative engagement opportunities Employee Discount From the first day of employment, colleagues gain access to LEGO s generous employee discount program. Performance Bonus Opportunities Eligible employees may participate in bonus programs when organizational goals and performance targets are achieved. Hybrid Working Environment LEGO supports flexible work arrangements through its hybrid working model. Employees generally work: Approximately three days per week in the office Remaining days remotely where role requirements allow The exact arrangement will be discussed during recruitment. Diversity and Inclusion at LEGO The LEGO Group is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels respected, valued, and empowered. Applications are encouraged from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of: Gender Gender identity Race Ethnicity National origin Religion Disability Sexual orientation Age LEGO believes diverse perspectives strengthen innovation and contribute to organizational success. Application Process Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. LEGO encourages interested students to apply as early as possible because positions may be filled before any advertised closing date. Candidates should prepare: Updated resume or CV. Academic information. Relevant skills and experience details. Supporting application documents requested through the LEGO Careers portal. Apply Here Why This Opportunity Matters For students interested in supply chain management, planning, operations, logistics, and business analytics, this role provides a valuable opportunity to gain experience with a globally recognized organization. Working within LEGO s Mould Supply Planning team allows students to develop practical skills, build professional networks, learn from experienced professionals, and gain insight into how a major international company manages complex operational processes. Students seeking meaningful professional experience while completing their studies should strongly consider applying for this opportunity. For more information, visit the official website. title: elea Talent Program 2027 in South Africa: Fully Immersive Global Career Opportunity in Impact Investing for University Graduates
summary: The elea Talent Program (eTP) 2027 is now accepting applications from ambitious and talented university graduates who are eager to build a meaningful career in impact investing, entrepreneurship, and international development. Organized by the elea Foundation for Ethics in Globalization, this prestigious two-year professional development program offers participants the opportunity to work alongside experienced investment professionals, gain exposure to entrepreneurial ecosystems across multiple continents, and contribute to solutions that address poverty and social challenges through business innovation. For the 2027 intake, elea is launching its first South African cohort, marking a major milestone in the organization’s global expansion strategy. The selected candidates will join the program in January 2027 and be based in Johannesburg, South Africa. If you are passionate about entrepreneurship, investment, innovation, and creating measurable social impact, the elea Talent Program could be the ideal stepping stone to an international career in impact investing. What is the elea Talent Program? The elea Talent Program is a structured two-year career development initiative designed to equip talented graduates with the skills, knowledge, and practical experience needed to thrive in the world of impact investing. Participants join elea as Associates and gain hands-on exposure to the organization’s investment activities, entrepreneurial support programs, and operational functions. Throughout the program, Associates work closely with startups, investors, and entrepreneurs while receiving continuous mentorship, coaching, and professional development support. The program provides a unique opportunity to understand how investments can be used as tools to create sustainable economic development and reduce poverty in emerging markets. About elea Foundation for Ethics in Globalization The elea Foundation for Ethics in Globalization is a Switzerland-based organization dedicated to fighting absolute poverty through entrepreneurship and impact investing. The organization supports promising early-growth companies that generate both financial sustainability and positive social impact. By investing in entrepreneurs who solve critical societal challenges, elea helps create jobs, improve livelihoods, and foster economic development in underserved regions around the world. The launch of the South African Talent Program cohort reflects elea’s commitment to developing local talent while strengthening its ability to identify and support impactful entrepreneurial solutions across Africa. Why the elea Talent Program Stands Out Unlike traditional graduate programs that focus on a single department or function, the elea Talent Program offers comprehensive exposure to the entire impact investing ecosystem. Participants gain practical experience across multiple aspects of elea’s operations, allowing them to develop a broad understanding of how investments, entrepreneurship, and social impact intersect. The program combines: Professional work experience International exposure Mentorship and coaching Skills development Leadership growth Cross-functional learning Global networking opportunities Key Development Goals of the Program The elea Talent Program is specifically designed to help participants build critical professional competencies. 1. Company Assessment Skills Participants learn how to evaluate businesses by analyzing: Business models Financial performance Market opportunities Growth potential Social impact outcomes 2. Understanding Investment Mechanisms Associates gain exposure to various investment approaches and learn how impact capital can be deployed to support entrepreneurial ventures. 3. Professional Skills Development The program emphasizes both technical and interpersonal skills through: Structured training programs On-the-job learning Professional coaching Mentorship sessions Practical assignments 4. Exposure to All Areas of elea Operations Participants develop a comprehensive understanding of: Investment processes Entrepreneur support Due diligence Portfolio management Impact measurement Organizational operations 5. Personal Growth and Career Development The program enables participants to discover their strengths and identify the specialization path that best aligns with their long-term career goals. International Exposure and Travel Opportunities One of the most attractive features of the elea Talent Program is its international dimension. Participants may have opportunities to travel within: Africa Asia Latin America Switzerland This global exposure allows Associates to learn directly from entrepreneurs, understand diverse markets, and develop a broader perspective on international business and impact investing. Who Should Apply? The elea Talent Program seeks highly motivated graduates who want to create meaningful impact through their professional careers. Ideal candidates are individuals who: Want to dedicate their talents to meaningful work Are interested in entrepreneurship and innovation Enjoy solving complex challenges Thrive in collaborative environments Demonstrate ambition and commitment Are eager to learn and grow professionally Have a global mindset Are open to international travel Eligibility Requirements To be considered for the 2027 cohort, applicants should meet the following requirements: Academic Qualifications Applicants must possess: A Bachelor’s degree or Honours degree An above-average academic record Professional Competencies Candidates should demonstrate: Excellent analytical skills Strong quantitative problem-solving abilities Ability to work effectively in teams Strong communication skills Intellectual curiosity Leadership potential Language Requirements Applicants must have: Strong English language proficiency Additional African languages are considered an advantage. Work Authorization Candidates must possess: A valid South African work permit Location Requirement Applicants must either: Be based in Johannesburg, South Africa, or Be willing to relocate to Johannesburg Leadership Experience While not mandatory, preference may be given to candidates who have demonstrated leadership through: Academic activities Professional experiences Community initiatives Extracurricular involvement Benefits of the elea Talent Program Participants selected for the program gain access to numerous professional and personal development opportunities. Benefits include: Two years of structured professional development Direct exposure to impact investing International work experience Travel opportunities across multiple continents Training and coaching from industry professionals Continuous mentorship and support Experience working with innovative entrepreneurs Development of analytical and investment skills Access to global networks Career advancement opportunities Exposure to early-growth ventures with measurable social impact Application Deadline The application window for the 2026 recruitment cycle is currently open. Application Deadline: 28 June 2026 Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline to maximize their chances of consideration. Apply Here Why Graduates Should Not Miss This Opportunity The elea Talent Program offers far more than a traditional graduate role. It provides participants with a rare opportunity to build expertise in impact investing while contributing to solutions that address some of the world s most pressing social and economic challenges. Graduates who aspire to work at the intersection of finance, entrepreneurship, and social impact will find the program particularly valuable. The combination of international exposure, professional development, mentorship, and meaningful work creates an exceptional foundation for a long-term career in the impact investing sector. For talented graduates seeking a purpose-driven career with global opportunities, the elea Talent Program 2027 represents an outstanding opportunity to learn, grow, and create lasting impact. For more information, visit the official website. title: Africa Women Writers Residency 2026: Fully Funded 4-Week Creative Writing Program for Emerging African Women Writers in Nairobi, Kenya
summary: The Africa Women Writers Residency 2026 is a fully funded literary development opportunity designed to empower emerging African women storytellers. This prestigious residency provides selected writers with the space, mentorship, training, and publishing platform needed to refine their craft and amplify their voices on an international stage. Organized by Majesty Publishing House Africa , the program aims to address the long-standing barriers that many African women writers face, including limited access to mentorship, publishing opportunities, and global literary networks. This initiative culminates in a published anthology titled “New African Voices: Stories That Shape Africa’s Future” , showcasing the works of selected participants. About the Africa Women Writers Residency 2026 The residency is a structured creative writing development program running in September 2026 . It is designed for twelve selected emerging women writers from across Africa. The program combines: Online intensive workshops and mentorship sessions Guest lectures by established African authors A fully funded in-person residency experience in Nairobi, Kenya Publication of selected works in a professional anthology The program supports writers working in multiple literary genres including: Fiction Fantasy Creative nonfiction Memoir writing Poetry This initiative is not only a training program but also a publication platform aimed at elevating African women’s literary voices globally. Program Structure and Format The residency is divided into two main phases: 1. Virtual Residency (3 Weeks) Participants will engage in: Interactive writing workshops One-on-one mentorship sessions Literary discussions and critiques Guest lectures from established authors Writing development exercises focused on thematic storytelling 2. In-Person Residency (1 Week in Nairobi, Kenya) Selected writers will travel to Nairobi for an immersive experience that includes: Intensive writing and revision sessions Cultural exchange activities Collaborative storytelling workshops Networking with African literary professionals Final editing and anthology preparation All costs are fully covered, ensuring equal access for selected participants. Funding and Support Coverage This is a fully funded residency. Selected participants will receive: International and local travel coverage Accommodation during the in-person residency Meals throughout the program Local transportation in Nairobi Cultural and learning activities Mentorship and publishing support This makes it one of the most accessible and impactful writing residencies for African women writers. Eligibility Criteria The program is open to emerging women writers from selected African countries. Eligible Countries: Kenya Uganda Ghana Nigeria South Africa Eligible Applicants: Applicants must: Identify as women writers Be emerging writers (published or unpublished) Not currently have literary representation Demonstrate proficiency in English Be able to commit fully to the program duration Eligible Writing Categories: Fiction Poetry Creative nonfiction Memoir Fantasy writing Application Requirements Interested applicants must submit the following: 1. Motivation Letter (250 words) Applicants must introduce themselves and explain: Their writing journey Their motivation for joining the residency Their goals as writers Their interest in African storytelling 2. Writing Sample (Theme-Based) Applicants must respond to the theme: “A New Dawn for Africa” Submission options include: A short story or essay (up to 1,000 words), OR One poem (up to 1,000 words), OR Two poems totaling approximately 1,000 words Why This Residency Matters The Africa Women Writers Residency plays a critical role in strengthening African literature by addressing structural barriers faced by women writers. Key benefits include: Professional literary mentorship Exposure to global publishing opportunities Development of storytelling skills Access to an African literary network Publication in a curated anthology Increased visibility for emerging writers By investing in women storytellers, the program ensures that African narratives are told authentically and reach global audiences. Program Location In-person residency location: Nairobi, Kenya Virtual participation: Open to selected candidates across eligible countries Deadline Application Deadline: 22 June 2026 Applications must be submitted via email before the deadline: residency@majestypublishingafrica.com Conclusion The Africa Women Writers Residency 2026 is a powerful opportunity for emerging African women writers to develop their craft, gain mentorship, and achieve publication in a professional anthology. With full funding and strong editorial support, this residency offers a rare chance to transform creative passion into professional literary success. Writers interested in participating should submit their materials to residency@ majestypublishingafrica.com before the deadline.
